slides.title: State in Flutter 🧠
slides.content:
# What is State?

**State** is data that can change over time and affects how the UI looks.

Examples: counter value, user authentication status, items in a shopping cart.

Types of state:
* **Ephemeral (local) state** – state owned by a single widget (e.g., selected tab).
* **App state** – state shared across many parts of the app (e.g., user info).

quizData.question: Which of the following is an example of app state?

options.text: Currently selected radio button in a dialog

quizData.explanation: App state is shared across many parts; user profile is a common example.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Start with local state

For simple cases, start with setState. Only introduce complex state management when needed (sharing across many widgets).
